Define `□` by `□b = b`, `□l0 = a`, `□l1 = a`, `□a = a`, `□t = a`; this is monotone, satisfies `⊠x ≤ □(⊠x)`, and makes the axiom `x ≤ □y -> x ≤ ⊠y -> x ≤ ⊠⊤` hold because anything below both `□y` and `⊠y` is already below `l0 = ⊠⊤`. Then `g = l0` is a Gödel fixpoint since `l0 ≡ ⊠l0 = l1`, but for `x = b` we get `⊠x = a` and `⊠(⊠x) = ⊠a = l0`, and `a` is not equivalent to `l0`.

  intro x y hxy hyx

The premise then says that from `x = y` and `y = x` we get `x ≡ y`, which is immediate.   intro x y z hxy hyz
